mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which directly expands corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within a monthly window.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts signal synchronized demand expansion across durable goods and real estate, which historically precedes positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative predictive power when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or rolling 3-year Spearman correlation drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surges indicate rapid capacity utilization improvements, which compress unit labor costs and boost profit margins, driving immediate equity gains over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0% YoY, or forward returns are flat across all triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth directly expands the aggregate corporate revenue base, translating into higher forward equity returns over a full quarter as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between GDP growth and SPX 60d returns remains below 0.1, or forward returns show no statistical edge over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces monetary tightening expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations within a monthly window, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite CPI acceleration exceeding 0.3% MoM for two consecutive months, or IC flips positive during high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market resilience, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative predictive power when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random walk over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp housing market contraction signals demand destruction and credit stress, which historically leads to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon during credit tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances despite housing declines, or signal shows no decay in predictive power across different rate environments.
